close_reason: Implemented migration to align confirm_story RPC signature/response with DAO contract; added migration validation test.
description:
Manual repro: in voice-assisted session, Select a Story -> Confirm triggers CONFIRMATION_FAILED with message: RPC failed: Could not find the function public.confirm_story(p_question_id, p_story_id) in the schema cache.

Findings:
- DAO calls supabase.rpc('confirm_story', { p_question_id, p_story_id }).
- Migration defines confirm_story(p_story_id text, p_claim_ids text[], p_new_status text default 'CONFIRMED'), so PostgREST cannot resolve requested arg names.
- DAO expects return shape { confirmed_story_id, excluded_count } but function currently returns { story_id, claims_updated, new_status }.
- Two newer migration files exist but are empty (0 bytes), suggesting missing follow-up migration.

Likely fix: add/replace SQL function to match API contract for path 313 (confirm selected story + exclude others by question), apply migration, and add migration-level verification test for arg names + return keys.
title: Fix confirm_story RPC signature mismatch causing story confirm failure

close_reason: All 4 critical issues resolved in plan: added 4 missing tables (verification_attempts, draft_metrics, draft_versions, story_metrics) to migration; clarified DraftData as local interface in StoryDAO.ts; disambiguated two TruthCheck types with explicit import paths; fixed test count from 330+ to ~3,145. Also addressed all warnings: added import paths for all error classes, supabaseUtils.ts adoption, .in() mock pattern, getSessionWithEvents join pattern, updateSessionAndStoryRecord compensation strategy, BackendErrors/PersistenceError naming collisions.
description:
4 critical issues found in majestic-dreaming-rose plan review:

1. Missing tables in migration: verification_attempts and draft_versions not in 28-table list
2. DraftData type doesn't exist — must resolve to actual schema name (Draft or DraftSchema)
3. Two conflicting TruthCheck schemas need disambiguation (TruthCheck.ts vs StoryRecord.ts)
4. Test count expectations wrong (3145 actual vs 330 stated)

Full review: ~/.claude/plans/majestic-dreaming-rose-REVIEW.md
title: Plan Review: Fix critical issues before DAO wiring implementation

close_reason: Completed
description:
## Summary
File upload-to-generation behavior regressed after merge reconciliation. The attach UI exists, but non-audio file content is no longer carried through to the `/api/generate` request, and attachment metadata/rendering from the previous pipeline is missing.

## User-Visible Regression
- Expected: uploaded text/image files are included in generation context and reflected in message metadata/UI.
- Actual: send flow ignores attachment payloads for generation path; attachment pipeline artifacts from prior implementation are absent.

## Prior Working Pattern (reference)
Canonical end-to-end implementation exists in commit `3675209`:
- Client-side payload prep and send wiring:
  - `frontend/src/lib/file-content.ts` (validation + payload transformation)
  - `frontend/src/app/page.tsx` (snapshot files, call `prepareFilesContent`, pass attachments to API)
  - `frontend/src/lib/api.ts` (3rd argument for attachments)
- Message metadata + rendering:
  - `frontend/src/lib/types.ts` (`attachments` on `Message`)
  - `frontend/src/components/chat/MessageBubble.tsx` (attachment list UI)
- Server-side ingestion:
  - `frontend/src/app/api/generate/route.ts` (attachment limits + user content assembly)
- Tests:
  - `frontend/__tests__/integration/file-send-flow.test.tsx`
  - `frontend/__tests__/api/generate-route.test.ts`

## Current Divergent Path
- Current `handleSendMessage` path does not prepare or pass attachment payloads.
- `frontend/src/lib/file-content.ts` is absent.
- `frontend/src/lib/api.ts` only sends `message` and `history`.
- `generate` route currently accepts no `attachments` field.

## Suspected Cause
Merge resolution dropped file-pipeline hunks (introduced in `3675209`) while retaining newer voice and generate-route changes, resulting in partial UI-only upload behavior.

## Scope
- Reintroduce attachment pipeline without regressing newer generate-route behavior (including current prompt/BAML constraints where applicable).
- Restore invariant: uploaded files attached at send time are represented in message state and API payload.
- Maintain existing audio transcription upload flow as-is.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Sending a message with uploaded supported files includes attachment payloads in `/api/generate` request.
2. User message stores attachment metadata and displays attached files in conversation UI.
3. Server route validates attachment limits and correctly folds text/image attachment content into model input.
4. Unsupported file types fail gracefully with user-visible error and do not crash send flow.
5. Coverage restored for end-to-end file-send flow and generate-route attachment handling.

## Verification Notes
- Manual check with at least one text file and one image file.
- Confirm no regressions to voice transcription upload (`/api/upload` + `/api/transcribe`) paths.
title: Regression: file upload content no longer reaches generation pipeline

close_reason: Fixed and committed: answer_session INIT no longer uses session UUID as questionId; ORIENT only runs with real question context
description: After fixing /api/sessions/undefined, session workflow for source=answer_session enters ORIENT stage and calls GET /api/story/orient-context?questionId=<session-uuid>. In SessionWorkflowShell, OrientStoryModule is invoked with questionId={session.id}. For answer_session this id is not a question id, causing 'Question not found: <sessionId>' errors. Need to either provide a real questionId in SessionView or avoid ORIENT stage for answer_session INIT.
notes:
Debug findings (2026-03-02): SessionWorkflowShell passes questionId={session.id} into OrientStoryModule. OrientStoryModule calls /api/story/orient-context?questionId=<that value>. For source=answer_session, GetSessionHandler returns only id/state/source and no questionId, so session UUID is used as questionId and ConfirmStoryDAO.findQuestionById misses with Question not found. Evidence: frontend/src/modules/session/SessionWorkflowShell.tsx line 81, frontend/src/data_loaders/loadOrientStoryData.ts line 25, frontend/src/app/api/story/orient-context/route.ts lines 28-31, frontend/src/server/request_handlers/GetSessionHandler.ts lines 11-17.
Reconfirmed on 2026-03-02 debug retry: /writer still routes to StartSessionRouteAdapter -> StartVoiceSessionModule -> createSession -> /session/:id. SessionWorkflowShell currently passes questionId={session.id} (frontend/src/modules/session/SessionWorkflowShell.tsx:81) into OrientStoryModule. That loader calls /api/story/orient-context?questionId=<session-id> (frontend/src/data_loaders/loadOrientStoryData.ts:25), and route throws DATA_NOT_FOUND Question not found for that UUID (frontend/src/app/api/story/orient-context/route.ts:28-31). GetSessionHandler returns SessionView without questionId (frontend/src/server/request_handlers/GetSessionHandler.ts:11-17; frontend/src/server/data_structures/SessionView.ts). answer_sessions schema has no question_id (supabase/migrations/20260302000000_initial_schema.sql:71-77). story_records table has question_id but createStoryRecord insert omits it (frontend/src/server/data_access_objects/SessionDAO.ts:141-143).
Implemented fix (2026-03-02): added optional questionId to SessionView/AnswerStoryRecord, mapped story_records.question_id in SessionDAO, and GetSessionHandler now returns questionId from story record for answer_session. Session stage mapping is now source-aware: INIT for answer_session without questionId maps to RECALL_REVIEW instead of ORIENT. SessionWorkflowShell now passes OrientStoryModule questionId={session.questionId} only when present and no longer uses session.id. Added unit coverage in modules/session tests for skip-orient and questionId prop wiring. Quality gates could not run in this environment because node/npm/npx are not installed.
title: Fix orient context query using sessionId as questionId

close_reason: Completed
description:
## Summary
After merge reconciliation, the assistant message **Edit** entrypoint is no longer connected to the voice-edit workflow. Clicking `Edit` in the assistant button ribbon opens the legacy text modal only, and there is no path from that entrypoint into realtime `voice_edit` mode.

## User-Visible Regression
- Expected: from message-level editing flow, voice edit mode is available/usable.
- Actual: `Edit` opens `EditMessageModal` (text-only), and voice mode is not reachable from that flow.

## Prior Working Pattern (reference)
- Voice-edit capability was introduced and verified in:
  - `7df1b55` (Voice Edit API + hook + panel)
  - `28c58b7` (page integration with voice controls)
  - `1800214` (stabilization of voice integration)
- Existing voice architecture is still present and should be reused:
  - `frontend/src/hooks/useVoiceEdit.ts`
  - `frontend/src/hooks/useRealtimeSession.ts`
  - `frontend/src/components/chat/VoiceEditPanel.tsx`

## Current Divergent Path
- Message-level edit button still routes to legacy modal flow:
  - `frontend/src/components/chat/ButtonRibbon.tsx` (`handleEditClick` → `setIsEditModalOpen(true)`)
  - `frontend/src/components/chat/EditMessageModal.tsx` (text-only UI)
- This bypasses `useVoiceEdit.startEditing()` and `VOICE_MODES.VOICE_EDIT`.

## Suspected Cause
Merge retained the legacy `ButtonRibbon/EditMessageModal` edit path without integrating the voice-edit entrypoint, so the voice implementation exists but is effectively disconnected for message-level edit action.

## Scope
- Reconnect message-level edit UX to voice edit mode (either mode picker or direct voice path with explicit fallback).
- Preserve existing non-voice editing behavior where intended.
- Ensure store updates and undo behavior still use the existing voice edit history flow.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Clicking assistant `Edit` provides a working path into voice edit mode (not text-only dead-end).
2. Voice edit path invokes `useVoiceEdit.startEditing()` and establishes realtime session with `VOICE_MODES.VOICE_EDIT`.
3. Edited content is applied through existing store update path and supports undo (`useConversationStore.popEdit`).
4. Automated coverage exists for message-level edit → voice mode path (unit/integration test).
5. Existing text edit behavior (if retained) remains functional and explicitly test-covered.

## Verification Notes
- Validate UI behavior manually on a live conversation with at least one assistant message.
- Confirm no regression to read-aloud controls while entering/exiting voice edit mode.
title: Regression: message-level Edit no longer exposes voice edit mode

Sub-slice of file-to-llm-pipeline models. Proposed change: support .txt/.doc/.docx/.pdf/.csv/.xls/.xlsx end-to-end.

description: Pushing to Dolt remote... fails in this repo. On bd 0.56.1 it failed with 'no store available'. After upgrading to 0.57.0 via doctor, failure is explicit: remote 'origin' not found in Dolt repo. Root cause appears to be missing Dolt remote configuration for database beads_silmari-writer on server 127.0.0.1:3307. Additional side effect: doctor started a second local Dolt server at 127.0.0.1:14274 with DB name 'dolt', causing metadata/DB name mismatch and potential issue visibility confusion.
notes:
Debug findings (2026-03-02):
- Reproduced both failures: bd dolt push/pull => fatal: remote 'origin' not found.
- Active server is systemd service dolt.service on 127.0.0.1:3307, WorkingDirectory=/home/maceo/.dolt-data/beads.
- beads_silmari-writer exists and is queryable; dolt_remotes table is empty.
- .beads/dolt-monitor.pid is stale, so 'bd dolt status' reports not running even though systemd server is active.
- MOTD script (/etc/update-motd.d/99-services-status) only reports service health; does not configure remotes.
- Cross-check against TROUBLESHOOTING#database-issues: local shadow DB exists at .beads/dolt with DB 'dolt' and 0 issues; active systemd server DB beads_silmari-writer has 24 issues. This matches documented stale shadow DB pattern, but active push failure remains missing Dolt remote on beads_silmari-writer.
title: Fix beads dolt push/pull remote configuration

close_reason: Fixed and pushed: SessionPage now resolves params safely and avoids /api/sessions/undefined requests
description: Writer flow creates a valid UUID session via POST /api/sessions, navigates to /session/{uuid}, then SessionPage calls getSession(params.sessionId) where params is not resolved in client runtime. This issues GET /api/sessions/undefined and returns 400 INVALID_REQUEST: Session ID must be a valid UUID. Reproduced locally with Playwright: POST /api/sessions => 200 {sessionId:<uuid>}, followed by GET /api/sessions/undefined (twice).
title: Fix /session route passing undefined sessionId to API

description: Main build fails after commit e1f2484: RecallScreen passes label/ariaLabel to RecordButton, but RecordButtonProps in commit HEAD does not include those props. Local working tree has an unstaged RecordButton.tsx update that adds label and ariaLabel and resolves this mismatch. Need to commit and push that file (or stop passing those props).
title: Fix RecordButton prop typing mismatch in Recall flow
